entries.text: Email sending capability added to Janet via SMTP through janet.bot88@gmail.com. Draft-first approach — always confirm with KS before sending.
KS plays polo every Sunday 7:55-9:00am — added as weekly recurring calendar event
Started Mounjaro last Tuesday (Feb 17). Felt nauseous during polo match today — likely combination of medication adjustment period and not eating enough before exercise. About 1 week into treatment.

I started taking Monjaro last Tuesday and noticed nausea during my polo match, possibly due to not eating enough before the game. I need to adjust my meal timing around the medication to avoid discomfort during physical activity.

Standardized on launchd (com.refinery.janetbot) as the sole way to run the bot — no more manual node bot.js starts. Added duplicate process guard to bot.js startup that kills any existing bot.js processes before initializing.
Cleared stale reminders (Improve Janet, Health Update). Set up weekly Mounjaro check-in at Sunday 6pm, daily 9pm Claude usage report (percentage only), and health check reminder for March 8.
Audit MCP connections and skills — currently only Oura and Granola. Evaluate Google Calendar, Gmail, GitHub, Google Drive, Brave Search MCPs for gaps in KS's workflow

Current custom integrations (calendar, email, search) are better than MCPs for context efficiency — MCPs add tool definitions to every conversation. Keep MCPs only for dynamic mid-conversation queries (Oura, Granola).

Janet must always present advantages AND disadvantages when proposing changes. Never suggest replacing something that already works unless the replacement is clearly superior. Lead with tradeoffs, not features.

KS approved weekly health trends correlation feature — cross-reference Oura sleep/readiness data with journal entries (late meals, stress, polo, Mounjaro) to identify personal patterns. Deliver Sunday 6pm with Mounjaro check-in.
YouTube video pipeline — download via yt-dlp, transcribe via Whisper locally on Mac Mini, then summarize/extract workflows via Claude. Would only capture audio, not visual content.
